title: Assigning tasks through email
excerpt: Hi I just checked with the support team. We can’t assign tasks directly through mails. Monday.com picks up specific keywords to do the automations. I work in an organization where we have 14000+ employees, not everyone w…

posts.author: Hitansh (Hitansh)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi I just checked with the support team. We can’t assign tasks directly through mails. Monday.com picks up specific keywords to do the automations. I work in an organization where we have 14000+ employees, not everyone would use that keyword in the mail. Now for those cases which don’t have that specific keyword, i have to manually type in that thing into monday.com. Can’t we assign someone that task through mail itself and it shows on Monday.com?

posts.author: MikeB_Axanexa (Mike Buckiewicz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi There!

There really isn’t a great way to assign people directly through the email.

However, you can use what would be call an Inbound board that houses the emails that don’t have key words and then set up conditions to move items from that board to the destination board based on other parameters or types.

Happy to talk ore about it and see what the options are if needed. You can contact me at mbuckiewicz@axanexa.com.

Mike B
Automation Architect
AXANEXA

posts.author: Kofi (Kofi FiftyTechSolutions.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
This could be done in a number a couple ways.
Option 1
Have a user send an email with an identifier such as “[023]” or [Mike2]" in the subject of the email. Then use something like Make.com to determine and assign the task to someone from the email.

Option 2
You could use Monday.com form with a field to of the name of the task owner. Then when add an automation in Monday to assign a person based on the field. However, limited to 40 people with status column. And 500 people using dropdown column. So option 1 would be a lot more robust.

Option 3
You could make a unique Monday link for each person that can be assigned a task. When a person emails that specific link. Monday automations would take over to create and assign the task. A bit complicated but also could work.

Feel free to reach out to me if you want more help on this or for me to implement.

Thanks,

Kofi
FiftyTechSolutions.com

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)

I would also suggest checking out Bitskout. Bitskout is a no-code app that integrates with monday and uses AI to simplify your processes.

It does too many things to list here, so you should check it out yourself.

Hope this helps!
Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com
